events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service - gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top. This is a classic instrumentation installation defect.

For gas service, impulse lines should slope upward from the pipe to the transmitter (so condensate drains back to pipe). For liquid service, impulse lines should slope downward from the pipe to the transmitter (so gas bubbles vent back to pipe).

The scenario says "gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top" which is backwards - gas taps are below the pipe (should be above) and liquid taps are on top (should be below). This is the classic wrong sloping.

This is a non-compliant, installed-defective condition. Decision should be fail since it's non-compliant.

Let me construct the JSON.

"Reconfigure impulse line take-offs and tubing runs so gas service taps are above the pipe and slope upward toward the transmitter",
"Reconfigure liquid service taps to be below the pipe and slope downward toward the transmitter",
"Verify slope is continuous with no sags or low/high points that could trap fluid or gas",
"Re-inspect against API RP 551 and manufacturer manuals before closeout"
],
"rationale": "Gas-service DP impulse lines must slope upward from the process to the transmitter so condensate drains back; liquid-service lines must slope downward so gas vents back. The observed condition is reversed for both, which will trap fluids/gas in the legs and produce measurement error. This is a non-compliant, installed-defective condition requiring rework before closeout.",
"references": ["API RP 551", "Manufacturer installation manuals"]
}
